title: Mapping gtp-u extension headers
abstract: Systems, methods, apparatuses, and computer program products for mapping General Packet Radio Service Tunneling Protocol User Plane (GTP-U) extension headers in Segment Routing version-6 (SRv6). One method may include receiving a data packet from a user equipment. The method may also include encapsulating the data packet into a general packet radio service tunneling protocol user plane (GTP-U) extension header. The method may further include mapping the GTP-U extension header to a segment routing internet protocol header via a time-length-value field. In addition, the method may include forwarding the GTP-U extension header along with the data packet to a user plane function element.
claims:
Claims WE CLAIM: 1. A method, comprising: receiving a data packet from a user equipment; encapsulating the data packet into a general packet radio service tunneling protocol user plane (GTP-U) extension header; mapping the GTP-U extension header to a segment routing internet protocol via a time-length-value field; and forwarding the GTP-U extension header along with the data packet to a user plane function element, wherein the mapping comprises mapping the GTP-U extension header to an encoding scheme of the segment routing internet protocol using the time- length-value field. 2. The method according to claim 1, wherein the segment routing internet protocol header carries control information. 3. The method according to claims 1 or 2, wherein the time-length- value comprises one or a combination of general packet radio service tunneling protocol user plane tunnel endpoint identifier, an element identifier, and an interface identifier. 4. The method according to any of claims 1-3, wherein the time-length- value field is applied in a segment routing header. 5. The method according to any of claims 1-4, wherein the GPT-U extension header is one of a radio access network type, an Xw radio acess network container type, a new radio access network container type, or a packet 

 data unit session container type. 6. The method according to any of claims 1-5, wherein the segment routing internet protocol header comprises an application identifier and a general packet radio service tunneling protocol user plane tunnel endpoint identifier. 7. The method according to any of claims 1-6, wherein the encapsulating is performed with the segment routing internet protocol header and the segment routing header. 8. The method according to any of claims 1-7, wherein the mapping further comprises mapping an error code and an end marker to the segment routing internet protocol header via the time-length-value field. 9. The method according to any of claims 1-8, wherein the mapping further comprises mapping a transfer protocol data unit (T-PDU) of the GTP- U to the segment routing internet protocol header via the time-length-value field. 10. A method, comprising: receiving, from a network element, a general packet radio service tunneling protocol user plane (GTP-U) extension header that is mapped to a segment routing internet protocol header, and comprising a data packet of a user equipment, extracting data from the GTP-U extension header by executing a segment identifier mobility function; and forwarding the GTP-U extension header to a data network. 11. The method according to claim 10, wherein extracting the data 

 comprises extracting GTP-U related content from the GTP-U extension header in the segment routing internet protocol header. 12. The method according to claims 10 or 11, wherein extracting the data comprises extracting a transfer protocol data unit (T-PDU) of GTP-U related content from the GTP-U extension header in the segment routing internet protocol header. 13. The method according to any of claims 10-12, further comprising processing the extracted GTP-U related content as per the GTP-U extension header. 14. The method according to any of claims 10-13, wherein the forwarding comprises forwarding the data packet according to local policies. 15. The method according to any of claims 10-14, wherein the data packet is forwarded to the data network according to an application identifier and a general packet radio service tunneling protocol user plane tunnel endpoint identifier located in a time-length-value field of the segment routing internet protocol header. 16. The method according to any of claims 10-15, wherein a transfer protocol data unit comprises the GTP-U extension header in a segment routing internet protocol header. 17. The method according to any of claims 10-16, wherein the segment routing internet protocol header carries control information. 18. The method according to any of claims 10-17, wherein the GTP-U extension header is one of a radio access network container type, an Xw radio 

 access network container type, a new radio access network container type, or a packet data unit session container type. 19. The method according to any of claims 10-18, wherein the GTP-U extension header is forwarded to the data network according to an application identifier and a general packet radio service tunneling protocol user plane tunnel endpoint identifier located in the GTP-U extension header. 20. The method according to any of claims 10-19, wherein the GTP-U extension header is received in a segment routing internet protocol environment. 21. An apparatus, comprising: at least one processor; and at least one memory comprising computer program code, the at least one memory and the computer program code are configured, with the at least one processor to cause the apparatus at least to receive, from a network element, a general packet radio service tunneling protocol user plane (GTP-U) extension header that is mapped to a segment routing internet protocol header, and comprising a data packet of a user equipment, extract data from the GTP-U extension header by executing a segment identifier mobility function; and forward the GTP-U extension header to a data network. 31.  means for receiving a data packet from a user equipment; means for encapsulating the data packet into a general packet radio service tunneling protocol user plane (GTP-U) extension header; means for mapping the GTP-U extension header to a segment routing internet protocol via a time-length-value field; and means for forwarding the GTP-U extension header along with the data packet to a user plane function element, wherein the mapping means comprises means for mapping the GTP-U extension header to an encoding scheme of the segment routing internet protocol using the time-length-value field. 42. A non-transitory computer readable medium comprising program instructions stored thereon for performing the method according to any of cl ims 1-20. 44.
